feature: Add employee search feature and salary info

issue.title: Add employee search feature and salary info

problem_statement: The payroll dashboard currently displays all employees in a single list without any filtering capabilities. As the employee roster grows, users need the ability to quickly find specific employees by name or title, and filter by salary ranges to identify compensation patterns. This feature will demonstrate the full spec-driven workflow while maintaining the existing CRUD functionality.

user_stories.i_want: to search employees by name or title
user_stories.so_that: I can quickly locate specific employees without scrolling through the entire roster

user_stories.i_want: to filter employees by minimum and maximum salary ranges
user_stories.so_that: I can identify employees within specific compensation bands for analysis and reporting

user_stories.i_want: to see which filters are currently active
user_stories.so_that: I understand what subset of employees I'm viewing and can easily adjust the criteria

user_stories.i_want: to clear all active filters with a single action
user_stories.so_that: I can quickly return to viewing the full employee list

must_have: Text search input that filters employees by name (case-insensitive partial match)
Text search input that filters employees by title (case-insensitive partial match)
Minimum salary filter input (numeric, filters employees with salary >= min value)
Maximum salary filter input (numeric, filters employees with salary <= max value)
Visual indicator showing currently active filters
Clear/reset button that removes all filters and returns to full employee list
Filters apply to both the employee directory table and the edit employee records section
All existing CRUD operations (create, update, delete) must continue to work unchanged
Filter state persists during page interactions (e.g., after creating/updating/deleting an employee)
Empty state message when no employees match the current filter criteria
should_have: Real-time filtering as the user types (debounced to avoid excessive rendering)
Display count of filtered results vs total employees
Filter inputs grouped together in a clear, accessible location on the dashboard
Responsive design that works on mobile and desktop viewports
nice_to_have: URL query parameters to preserve filter state across page reloads
Keyboard shortcuts to quickly focus on search inputs
Filter presets (e.g., 'High earners', 'Entry level') for common salary ranges
Highlight matching text in search results

acceptance_criteria: Verify that entering text in the name search field shows only employees whose names contain that text (case-insensitive)
Verify that entering text in the title search field shows only employees whose titles contain that text (case-insensitive)
Verify that entering a minimum salary value shows only employees with salaries at or above that amount
Verify that entering a maximum salary value shows only employees with salaries at or below that amount
Verify that multiple filters can be applied simultaneously and results reflect all active criteria (AND logic)
Verify that clicking the clear/reset button removes all filter values and displays the full employee list
Verify that active filters are visually indicated on the page (e.g., highlighted inputs, filter summary, or badge count)
Verify that when no employees match the filter criteria, an appropriate empty state message is displayed
Verify that creating a new employee while filters are active adds the employee to the database and the filtered view updates accordingly
Verify that updating an employee's information while filters are active reflects the changes and re-evaluates the filter criteria
Verify that deleting an employee while filters are active removes the employee and updates the filtered view
Verify that the summary statistics (headcount, total payroll, average salary, individual contributors) update based on the filtered employee set
out_of_scope: Advanced search operators (e.g., boolean queries, regular expressions)
Sorting functionality (e.g., sort by name, salary, or title)
Pagination or infinite scroll for large employee lists
Export filtered results to CSV or other formats
Saved filter presets or user-specific filter preferences
Multi-select filters (e.g., filtering by multiple specific titles)
Filter by manager, home address, or other employee fields not mentioned in requirements
Backend API changes (filtering should be implemented client-side for this demo)
Authentication or role-based access control for filter visibility
open_questions: Should the search be case-sensitive or case-insensitive? (Recommendation: case-insensitive for better UX)
Should the salary filters accept decimal values or only whole numbers? (Current DB schema uses INTEGER for salary)
Where should the filter controls be positioned on the page? (Options: above employee directory table, in sidebar, or in a dedicated filter panel)
Should filters update in real-time as the user types, or require a submit button? (Recommendation: real-time with debouncing for better UX)
Should the summary statistics (headcount, payroll totals) reflect the filtered subset or always show totals for all employees? (Recommendation: update to show filtered subset for consistency)
Should partial matches work for both name and title search? (Recommendation: yes, using substring matching)
